nativeRange.habitat: Generally found on clear lagoon and seaward reefs, typically occurring in loose aggregations near caves and ledges. Has been documented as abundant over branching corals and on leeward coasts, at depths ranging from approximately 11 to 120 m.

diet.notes: Planktivorous in the wild (trophic level ~3.0), feeding on zooplankton in midwater aggregations above reef structures. In captivity has been reported to accept a varied diet of meaty frozen foods (mysis shrimp, brine shrimp), herbivore flakes, and frozen preparations. Vitamin-enriched foods are generally advised to help maintain colouration.

summary: A small, peaceful schooling damselfish from the Indian Ocean reef, recognised by its golden-yellow body and violet-pink facial markings. Generally considered reef-safe and well-suited to groups of three or more in an established reef aquarium.
careNotes:
The Agile Chromis is endemic to the Indian Ocean, ranging from the East African coast (Kenya to Mozambique), Madagascar, and the Comoro Islands to the Maldives, Seychelles, and Chagos Archipelago. FishBase lists the current accepted name as Pycnochromis agilis (reclassification from Chromis agilis); the trade name Chromis agilis remains in common hobby use.

In the wild this species tends to form loose aggregations in the mid-water column near caves, ledges, and branching corals on clear lagoon and seaward reefs, typically at 11–120 m depth.

Adult size: LiveAquaria states a maximum of approximately 3 inches (7.6 cm TL); FishBase records a maximum of 6.1 cm SL (standard length), which corresponds to roughly 6.7–7.0 cm TL using a typical SL-to-TL conversion for pomacentrids. These figures are broadly consistent; 7.6 cm is used as the upper bound.

Temperament: The LiveAquaria collection page categorises some chromis as semi-aggressive in filter metadata, but the species-specific product description notes a peaceful demeanour with mild territorial behaviour primarily toward conspecifics in undersized groups or tanks with insufficient hiding places. Aggression in captivity has been reported to be more pronounced in small groups; keeping three or more individuals with adequate rockwork tends to disperse dominance interactions.

Tank requirements: LiveAquaria lists a 30-gallon (approximately 114 L) minimum for a specimen. Because the species is a schooling fish that requires at least three individuals and needs open mid-water swimming space combined with multiple retreat areas, 120 L is used as the minimum to accommodate a small school comfortably.

Reproduction: Oviparous with distinct pair bonding during breeding. Eggs are demersal and adhere to hard substrate; males guard and aerate the clutch. Captive spawning has not been widely documented in the hobby literature reviewed.

Diet notes: In the wild the species has been recorded with a trophic level of approximately 3.0, consistent with omnivory and planktivory. In captivity it has been reported to accept a broad range of prepared and frozen foods readily, making it generally considered an easy feeder.
breedingNotes: Oviparous with distinct pair bonding during breeding. Demersal adhesive eggs are deposited on hard substrate and guarded and aerated by the male. Captive breeding is rarely documented in the hobby literature.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary husbandry reference: max size 3 in (7.6 cm), minimum tank 30 gal (114 L), temperature 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C), pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, care level easy, reef compatible yes, diet omnivore (meaty items, herbivore flakes, frozen preparations), temperament peaceful, group of 3 or more recommended. Note: initial fetch of the collection page returned only aggregate filter data; the direct product URL yielded full care details.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Pomacentridae, Ovalentaria/misc; reclassified as Pycnochromis agilis), native range (Indian Ocean: Maldives, Chagos, Seychelles, Madagascar, Comoros, East African coast), maximum length 6.1 cm SL, depth range 11–120 m, habitat (clear lagoon and seaward reefs, near caves and branching corals), trophic level 3.0 (omnivore/planktivore), oviparous breeding with male egg-guarding, IUCN Least Concern. No water parameter data (temperature, pH, salinity) provided by FishBase for this species — husbandry parameters sourced from LiveAquaria only.

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous; distinct pair bonding during breeding. Demersal adhesive eggs guarded and aerated by the male. Captive spawning is rarely documented and rearing pelagic larvae is considered difficult.
